query_text: What challenge does the OECD review identify in aligning sustainability and efficiency with resilience?

retrieved_chunks.title: OECD Supply Chain Resilience Review: Navigating Risks
retrieved_chunks.text: 132  OECD SUPPLY CHAIN RESILIENCE REVIEW © OECD 2025 In addition to the specific policies outlined in the previous chapter, in order to promote agile, adaptable and aligned supply chains, policy design must account for the interplay between resilience and other objectives, including sustainability and efficiency. Policymakers may face the challenge of aligning these objectives in ways that foster long-term economic stability and sustainable development while ensuring the ca pacity to withstand disruptions. While it is possible to achieve synergies across these dimensions, trade -offs are also inherently involved. This chapter examines these issues through a policy lens. 7.1. Synergies between resilience, efficiency and sustainability can be captured From the perspective of governments regulating supply chains, many policies not only strengthen the capacity to withstand disruptions, but also contribute to sustainability and efficiency. retrieved_chunks.text:  131 OECD SUPPLY CHAIN RESILIENCE REVIEW © OECD 2025 The previous chapter has listed specific policy actions and areas that can underpin resilient supply chains. This chapter takes a more strategic overview, exploring how policy design needs to balance sustainability, efficiency and resilience, while dealing with trade-offs among them. 7 Policies can balance sustainability, efficiency and resilience
